steps.action: Signed into the platform dashboard with the seeded admin account.
steps.result: Admin session token was issued successfully and cross-restaurant control is available.

steps.action: Opened Restaurants, clicked Add Restaurant, entered `Harbor Ember Kitchen`, selected `restaurant`, and clicked Save.
steps.result: The new restaurant was created and became available for branch, owner, menu, and employee setup.

steps.action: Opened Branches, clicked Add Branch three times, and created Downtown Flagship, New Cairo Studio, and Maadi Terrace for the new restaurant.
steps.result: All three operational branches were created and linked to Harbor Ember Kitchen.

steps.action: Opened Users and provisioned the owner, stakeholder, supervisor, waiter, cashier, and kitchen users for the new restaurant.
steps.result: The restaurant now has authenticated accounts for admin control, owner oversight, and branch operations on the waiter, cashier, kitchen, and owner apps.

steps.action: Signed into the owner dashboard for Harbor Ember Kitchen.
steps.result: Owner-scoped session is active and restricted to the new restaurant.

steps.action: Opened Categories, Products, Menus, Tables, Inventory, and Employees to create the launch catalog, dining room map, team records, and low-stock monitoring for each branch.
steps.result: Each branch now has a working menu, sellable products, dine-in tables, employee records, and inventory thresholds for the first trading day.

29.menu_name: New Cairo Studio All Day Menu

steps.action: Logged into the customer app with a new phone number and opened the home screen.
steps.result: The new restaurant appears in the marketplace listing, ready for discovery before the first dine-in order is placed.

steps.action: Opened T1 Garden, attached Mona Sami to the table, added full meals and drinks with modifiers, then saved the dine-in order.
steps.result: Order #244 was created on Downtown Flagship and the table became occupied.

items.change_note: One without onion on the pass.
items.item_note: One without onion on the pass.

items.change_note: Less sweet for the second cup.
items.item_note: Less sweet for the second cup.

steps.action: Added dessert to the same open table and sent the full ticket to the kitchen display.
steps.result: Kitchen now has a queued dine-in ticket with mains, drinks, and dessert for Mona Sami.

steps.action: Reduced the latte quantity after the guests changed their mind, then refunded the brownie that the table no longer wanted.
steps.result: The live order total was recalculated and the refunded dessert dropped out of active service.

steps.action: Opened the KDS board, advanced the active items from queued to preparing, then to ready.
steps.result: All non-refunded lines for Mona’s order became ready for cashier handoff.

items.item_note: One without onion on the pass.
items.change_note: One without onion on the pass.

items.item_note: Less sweet for the second cup.

steps.action: Pulled Mona’s ready order into cashier, confirmed the final total, and completed payment by card.
steps.result: The order closed as paid, the table reopened, and Mona earned loyalty points for the completed visit.

items.change_note: One without onion on the pass.
items.item_note: One without onion on the pass.
Less sweet for the second cup.

steps.actor_name: Karim Adel / Tamer Wael / Salma Nasser
steps.action: Processed a drinks-only dine-in visit from order creation through kitchen readiness and cash settlement.
steps.result: The first branch now has both card and cash sales on the same trading day.

steps.actor_name: Karim Adel / Tamer Wael / Salma Nasser
steps.action: Moved a family table mid-service from T3 Family to T4 Lounge, reduced a drink line, completed kitchen production, and settled the final bill with split payment.
steps.result: The system preserved the audit trail of the table move while still allowing the visit to finish and pay successfully.

steps.action: Processed light paid service in the second and third branches so the owner can review branch comparison, payment mix, and cross-branch performance on the first day.
steps.result: The restaurant now has paid activity across all branches for the end-of-day owner review.

steps.action: Reopened the customer app after payment to check visit history and loyalty.
steps.result: The first dine-in visit now appears in order history with updated loyalty points and the correct restaurant and branch names.

steps.action: Opened the live dashboard at close of day to review revenue, orders, payment mix, branch performance, low stock, and recent orders.
steps.result: The dashboard reflects the completed first day across three branches, including paid revenue, mixed tenders, loyalty activity, and low-stock warnings.
